source.zh: 《古兰经》（第2、7、11、15、20、21、29、38、41、50、55、65、67、72 章等）；布哈里与穆斯林两大圣训集；塔巴里《历代先知与帝王史》卷一（10 世纪）；「先知故事」传统：萨阿拉比《集会之新娘》（11 世纪）、基萨伊《先知故事》；宇宙志：卡兹维尼《创造物之奇迹与万物之异象》（13 世纪）、伊本·瓦尔迪《奇迹之珍珠》（15 世纪）；前伊斯兰宗教：伊本·凯勒比《偶像之书》（9 世纪）
source.en: The Qur'an (suras 2, 7, 11, 15, 20, 21, 29, 38, 41, 50, 55, 65, 67, 72 and others); the hadith collections of al-Bukhari and Muslim; al-Tabari, Ta'rikh al-rusul wa'l-muluk, vol. 1 (10th c.); the Qisas al-Anbiya tradition — al-Tha'labi, 'Ara'is al-majalis (11th c.), and al-Kisa'i; for the cosmography, al-Qazwini, 'Aja'ib al-makhluqat (13th c.) and Ibn al-Wardi, Kharidat al-'aja'ib (15th c.); for pre-Islamic religion, Ibn al-Kalbi, Kitab al-Asnam (9th c.)

note.zh:
前伊斯兰的阿拉伯半岛没有留下任何成系统的创世叙事。我们对那个时代宗教的了解来自三处：《古兰经》对拉特、欧萨、麦那三位女神的提及（53:19–20）、伊本·凯勒比《偶像之书》所记的胡巴勒等神像与部落祭仪、以及大量的贾希利亚诗歌——这三类材料里都没有天地起源的故事。市面上标着「古代阿拉伯创世神话」的说法，基本是近代的拼凑。下面这条时间线是伊斯兰的。

《古兰经》本身也不是按顺序讲创世的：相关经文散在几十章里，多为向已知故事的听众所作的提示，而非连贯叙事。这条线索的排列顺序，依照的是塔巴里《历代先知与帝王史》和后世「先知故事」（Qisas al-Anbiya）这类整理性著作的通行次序。

经文内部有若干长期被讨论的地方。六日创造：41:9–12 分开来算是「二日造地、四日定给养、二日造七天」，加起来是八日，注经家的通行解法是把「四日」读成包含前面的两日。天地的先后是另一处旧案：2:29 与 41:9–12 是先地后天，79:27–30 却先把天建起来，然后才说「此后，他把大地伸展开」；伊本·阿拔斯被问到这一点时，据传答以地的实体先造、而铺展与给养在造天之后，这一读法后来成为通行解。「日」（yawm）通常被读作一段时期，22:47 说主那里的一日如你们所数的一千年，70:4 又说是五万年。与《创世记》最显眼的差别是没有第七日安息——50:38 明说创造六日而真主未感疲倦。造阿丹的原料，不同章节用了不同的词：土（turab，3:59）、泥（tin，6:2）、黑色成形的黏土（salsal min hama' masnun，15:26）、陶器般的干泥（salsal ka'l-fakhkhar，55:14），传统注经把它们读成同一过程的几个阶段，而不是互相冲突的版本。

人物细节也要分层看。阿丹妻子的名字「哈娃」从未出现在《古兰经》里，经文只称「他的妻子」；肋骨造女人出自圣训，不出自经文。二人一同受引诱、一同承认自己自欺（7:23），阿丹随后奉到从主降示的几句话而得宽恕（2:37），因此伊斯兰传统里没有原罪教义。伊卜劣厮的身份自古有争议：18:50 明说他「本是精灵」，但由于叩拜之命是对众天使下的，哈桑·巴士里等一系注经家仍主张他原是天使而堕落，两说并存至今。至于阿丹身高六十肘、天使从大地四方取来不同颜色的泥土因而人有不同肤色、伊卜劣厮绕着尚未有灵的泥像发现它中空——这些具体细节来自圣训与「先知故事」，其中不少属于所谓「以色列传闻」（isra'iliyyat），即经由犹太教、基督教传统进入的材料，伊本·凯西尔等后世学者对此持保留态度。

最后一步的卡夫山与巴哈姆特不是教义。七层天与七层地出自经文（2:29、65:12），但环绕大地的绿宝石之山、层层相驮的天使—宝岩—巨牛—巨鱼，出自「奇迹志」（'aja'ib）体裁的民间宇宙志，卡兹维尼与伊本·瓦尔迪是其代表；牛与鱼的名字在不同抄本里各不相同，这套图景从未进入信条。
note.en:
Pre-Islamic Arabia left no systematic cosmogony at all. What is known of its religion comes from three places — the Qur'an's mentions of the goddesses Allat, al-'Uzza and Manat (53:19–20); Ibn al-Kalbi's Book of Idols, which catalogues Hubal and the tribal cults; and the large surviving body of pre-Islamic poetry — and none of the three tells how the world began. Anything sold as an 'ancient Arabian creation myth' is a modern reconstruction. The timeline below is Islamic.

The Qur'an does not narrate creation in sequence either. The material is scattered across dozens of suras, mostly as allusions to a story the hearers were assumed to know. The order followed here is the one used by the later compilers who did lay it out as a sequence: al-Tabari's History and the Qisas al-Anbiya (Stories of the Prophets) tradition.

Several points inside the text have been argued over for centuries. On the six days: 41:9–12, taken literally, gives two days for the earth, four for its provisions, and two for the seven heavens — eight in all — and the standard exegetical fix is to read the four as inclusive of the first two. The order of earth and sky is another old crux: 2:29 and 41:9–12 put the earth first, while 79:27–30 builds the heaven and only afterwards says that He spread the earth out. Ibn 'Abbas, asked about this, is reported to have answered that the earth was made first but was spread out and provisioned after the heavens, and that reading became the standard one. 'Day' (yawm) is generally read as a period, and its length is given differently in 22:47 (a thousand years of your reckoning) and 70:4 (fifty thousand). The clearest contrast with Genesis is the absence of a seventh-day rest: 50:38 states that God made the heavens and the earth in six days and no weariness touched Him. The stuff Adam was made from is named with different words in different verses — dust (turab, 3:59), clay (tin, 6:2), dried clay from dark mud (salsal min hama' masnun, 15:26), clay like pottery (salsal ka'l-fakhkhar, 55:14) — which the commentators read as stages of one process rather than as rival accounts.

The personal detail has to be sorted by layer. Adam's wife is never named in the Qur'an, only called 'his wife'; the name Hawwa and the rib both come from hadith. They are tempted together, both say 'we have wronged ourselves' (7:23), and Adam is then given words from his Lord and forgiven (2:37), so there is no doctrine of original sin. The status of Iblis has been disputed since early times: 18:50 says plainly that he was of the jinn, but because the command to prostrate was addressed to the angels, exegetes in the line of Hasan al-Basri held that he was a fallen angel, and both positions are still current. Details like Adam's height of sixty cubits, the clay gathered in different colours from the four quarters of the earth, or Iblis walking around the lifeless figure and finding it hollow come from hadith and from the Qisas — a stratum that includes a good deal of isra'iliyyat, material drawn from Jewish and Christian tradition, which later scholars such as Ibn Kathir treated with suspicion.

The last stage is not doctrine. The seven heavens and seven earths are scriptural (2:29, 65:12), but the emerald mountain ringing the world and the stack of angel, rock, bull and fish belong to the popular 'aja'ib cosmographies of al-Qazwini and Ibn al-Wardi. The bull and the fish are given different names in different manuscripts, and the picture never entered the creed.

title.zh: 宝座在水上，天还是一团烟
title.en: The Throne upon the water, the sky still smoke

text.zh: 经文不从虚无讲起。11:7 说真主在六日内创造了天地，「那时，他的宝座是在水上的」——水在开篇时已经在那里，从何而来，经文不说。另一处（41:11）说，真主转而造天，而天当时还是一团烟；他对天与地说，你们俩顺服地或勉强地来吧，天与地回答，我们顺服地来了。创造在这里靠的是命令：真主判决一件事，只对它说「有」，它就有了（2:117）。太初没有对手，没有搏斗，也没有被杀死的怪物。
text.en: The Qur'an does not begin from nothing. In 11:7 God makes the heavens and the earth in six days, and His Throne was upon the water — the water is simply there on the first line, and where it came from is not said. In 41:11 He turns to the sky while it is still smoke, and tells the sky and the earth to come, willingly or unwillingly; they answer that they come willingly. Creation works by command: when God decrees a thing He says to it 'Be', and it is (2:117). There is no adversary at the outset, no combat, no slain monster.

title.zh: 天地本是闭塞的一块，被劈开
title.en: Heaven and earth were sewn shut, and were ripped open

text.zh: 21:30 是最接近开辟母题的一句：「不信道者难道不知道吗？天地原是闭塞的（ratq），而我把它们开辟（fatq）了；我用水创造一切生物。」经文只给了这一下动作，没有说谁站在两者之间把它们推开。ratq 与 fatq 这一对词，本义是「缝合」与「撕开」，注经家有的读作天地原本相连而后分离，有的读作天原本不下雨、地原本不长草，是这一劈才让水和芽出来。
text.en: The closest thing to a separation motif is 21:30: have the disbelievers not seen that the heavens and the earth were a closed mass (ratq), and We split them apart (fatq), and We made every living thing from water. That is the whole of the action, and no one stands between the two to push them apart. The pair of words means, at root, 'sewn shut' and 'torn open', and the commentators divide over what was opened: some read heaven and earth as once joined and then parted, others as a sky that would not rain and a ground that would not sprout until the split let the water and the shoots through.

title.zh: 七层天，山如楔子钉住大地
title.en: Seven heavens, and mountains driven in like pegs

text.zh: 真主在六日内造了天地，随后升上宝座（7:54）。次序是先地后天：他先造了大地上的一切，再转向天，把它造成七层（2:29）；另一处说他造了七层天和同样层数的大地（65:12）。大地被铺开（79:30），山被立起来当楔子（78:7），免得它随着人摇晃（16:15）；河流与道路是随之开出来的。最下一层天用灯饰点缀，那些灯同时是打向恶魔的投掷物（67:5）。六日之后没有安息：50:38 明说真主创造六日而未觉一丝疲倦。
text.en: God makes the heavens and the earth in six days, then mounts the Throne (7:54). The order is earth first: He creates what is on the earth, then turns to the sky and fashions it into seven heavens (2:29); elsewhere He is said to have made seven heavens and as many earths (65:12). The earth is spread out (79:30) and mountains are set into it as pegs (78:7) so that it will not sway with its people (16:15); rivers and roads are cut afterwards. The nearest heaven is hung with lamps, which double as missiles thrown at the devils (67:5). Nothing follows the sixth day: 50:38 says the work took six days and that no weariness touched Him.

title.zh: 以无烟之火造精灵，以光造天使
title.en: Jinn out of scorching fire, angels out of light

text.zh: 15:27 说：在造人之前，真主曾用「热风之火」造了精灵（jinn，亦音译作镇尼）；55:15 说得更具体——用一股无烟的纯焰。穆斯林《圣训实录》里的一条把三族并列：天使由光造，精灵由无烟的火焰造，阿丹由已经告诉你们的那样东西造。精灵先住在地上，与人一样吃喝、婚配、生死、分部落，有信道的也有不信道的（第72章「精灵章」）。它们那时还能升到天的座位上偷听天上的消息，直到守卫与流星把它们赶下来（72:8–9）。注经与「先知故事」传统还讲，先住地上的精灵在那里作恶流血，被逐往海岛与山中——天使后来质问真主「你要在大地上设置作恶流血者吗」（2:30），常被解释为指这件旧事。
text.en: In 15:27 the jinn are made before man, out of the fire of the scorching wind; 55:15 is more precise, a pure flame with no smoke in it. A hadith in Sahih Muslim sets the three orders side by side: the angels were created from light, the jinn from a smokeless flame, and Adam from what has already been described to you. The jinn lived on the earth first. They eat, marry, breed and die, they are divided into tribes, and some of them believe and some do not (sura 72). They could still climb to stations in the sky and listen in on it, until guards and shooting stars began driving them off (72:8–9). Commentary and the Qisas add that the jinn on earth fell to shedding blood there and were driven out to the islands and the mountains — which is how the angels' question in 2:30, will You put on the earth one who will spread corruption and shed blood, is usually explained.

title.zh: 陶器一样的干泥，敲上去会响
title.en: Dry clay like pottery, and it rang when struck

text.zh: 真主对众天使说：我必定在大地上设置一个代治者（2:30）。他用陶器般的干泥造人（55:14），那泥又被称作黑色的、成形的黏土（15:26），他亲手塑造（38:75），然后把自己的精神吹进去（15:29）。穆斯林《圣训实录》记，泥像还没有灵的时候摆在那里，伊卜劣厮绕着它走，发现它是中空的，断定这东西管不住自己。人立起来后，真主把万物的名称都教给了阿丹，再让天使说，天使说不出（2:31），阿丹一一说出来。布哈里的圣训说阿丹身高六十肘，此后的人一代比一代矮。
text.en: God tells the angels that He is placing a khalifa, a deputy, on the earth (2:30). He shapes the man from dry clay like potter's ware (55:14) — elsewhere dark mud moulded into form (15:26) — makes him with His own hands (38:75), and breathes into him of His spirit (15:29). A hadith in Sahih Muslim has the figure lying there before the spirit is in it, with Iblis circling it, finding it hollow, and concluding that a creature made like that will not hold itself together. Once Adam stands up, God teaches him the names of all things and then asks the angels for them; the angels cannot answer (2:31), and Adam names them one by one. Al-Bukhari reports that Adam was made sixty cubits tall, and that men have been shrinking ever since.

title.zh: 伊卜劣厮说：你用火造我，用泥造他
title.en: Iblis: You made me from fire and him from clay

text.zh: 真主命众天使向阿丹叩拜，众天使都拜了，只有伊卜劣厮不肯（2:34）。他的理由是：我比他优越，你用火造我，用泥造他（7:12）。18:50 说他本是精灵，所以违抗了主的命令。他被逐出，求宽限到复活之日，得到允许（7:14–15），随即立誓要坐在真主的正路上，从前后左右来引诱人（7:16–17）。阿丹与他的妻子住在乐园里，可以随意吃，只有一棵树不可近。撒旦劝他们，说这是长生树，是不朽的国权（20:120）；二人吃了，阴部随即显露，只好扯乐园的树叶来遮身（7:22）。他们说：主啊，我们已自欺了。阿丹奉到从主降示的几句话，得到宽恕（2:37）。真主叫他们下去，互相仇视，大地上有你们暂时的住处和享受（2:36）。
text.en: God orders the angels to prostrate themselves before Adam. They all do, except Iblis (2:34). His reason: I am better than he is; You made me from fire and made him from clay (7:12). Sura 18:50 says he was of the jinn, and so disobeyed his Lord's command. Expelled, he asks for respite until the Day of Resurrection and is granted it (7:14–15), then swears he will sit in wait on God's straight path and come at people from before them and behind them, from their right and their left (7:16–17). Adam and his wife are put in the garden and may eat freely, except from one tree. Satan whispers to them that it is the tree of everlasting life and of a kingdom that never decays (20:120). They eat; their nakedness is exposed to them, and they start stitching the leaves of the garden over themselves (7:22). Both say: our Lord, we have wronged ourselves. Adam receives words from his Lord and is forgiven (2:37). They are sent down as enemies to one another, with a dwelling on the earth and enjoyment of it for a while (2:36).

title.zh: 炉灶冒出水来，船停在朱迭山
title.en: The oven boiled over, and the ship came to rest on al-Judi

text.zh: 努哈在族人中间传道，「一千年，只差五十年」（29:14），无人听。他奉命造船，头人们从他身旁走过就嘲笑他（11:38）。约定的信号是一句怪话：等到我的命令降临，「炉灶（tannur）沸溢」的时候，就带上每种生物各一对，和你的家属上船——但那些已被判决的人除外（11:40）。注经家至今争论 tannur 究竟是一口真的烤炉、还是地面、还是水涌出的那个地方。努哈的一个儿子不肯上船，说我要爬上一座山去躲水；努哈喊，今天除了真主所怜悯的人，谁也保护不了谁——一个浪头打在父子之间，他淹死了（11:42–43）。随后一声令下：地啊，吸干你的水；天啊，止住你的雨。水退了，船停在朱迭山上（11:44）。真主对努哈说，他不是你的家属，他的行为不端。
text.en: Nuh preaches among his people for a thousand years less fifty (29:14) and is not heard. He is told to build the ship, and the chiefs laugh at him every time they pass by (11:38). The agreed signal is a strange one: when the command comes and the oven — the tannur — boils over, load a pair of every kind, and your household, except those already sentenced (11:40). Whether the tannur is a literal baking oven, the surface of the ground, or the spot where the water first welled up is still argued over. One of Nuh's sons refuses to board and says he will climb a mountain and be safe from the water; Nuh calls back that today nothing shields anyone from God's command but His mercy — and a wave comes between them, and the son is drowned (11:42–43). Then the word goes out: earth, swallow your water; sky, hold back. The water sinks, and the ship comes to rest on al-Judi (11:44). God tells Nuh that the boy was not of his household: his conduct was not righteous.

phase.en: The present world and the Hour

title.zh: 驮世界的鱼，和它终结的号角
title.en: The fish that carries the world, and the trumpet that ends it

text.zh: 经文给出的世界结构是七层天与七层地。中世纪的民间宇宙志再往下画：大地由一位天使背着，天使脚下要有立处，于是有一块宝石巨岩；岩下是一头巨牛（常称库尤萨），牛立在一条大鱼的背上——那就是巴哈姆特，鱼下是水，水下是风，风下是幽暗，再往下人的知识就断了。环绕人间的大洋之外还有一圈山，卡兹维尼说它由绿宝石构成，天空的青色是它的反光，山那边住着镇尼。这一层出自「奇迹志」体裁，不是教义。至于这个世界的终点，经文说得很清楚：号角一响，天上地上的一切都昏倒，再响一次，众人站起来观望（39:68）；群山那时像弹松的彩毛（101:5），大地被换成另一个大地（14:48）。
text.en: Scripture gives the shape of the world as seven heavens and seven earths. The medieval cosmographers kept drawing downward: the earth is carried by an angel, the angel needs something to stand on, so there is a rock of jewel beneath him; under the rock a vast bull, often called Kuyutha; the bull stands on the back of a fish, and the fish is Bahamut; under the fish, water; under the water, wind; under the wind, darkness, and there human knowledge stops. Beyond the ocean that rings the inhabited world stands another ring, Mount Qaf, which al-Qazwini describes as green emerald whose reflection is the blue of the sky, with the jinn living on the far side of it. That layer belongs to the 'aja'ib books, not to doctrine. About the end, the text is exact: the trumpet is blown and everyone in the heavens and on the earth falls senseless, and it is blown again and they rise, looking on (39:68); the mountains go like tufts of carded wool (101:5), and the earth is exchanged for another earth (14:48).
